section: JEF P104 – A Question of Trust
title: A Question of Trust – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Victor Canning

questions.question: Who is Horace Danby?
questions.correct_answer: A respectable locksmith who secretly steals
questions.explanation: Horace appears honest but lives a double life as a professional thief.

questions.question: What does Horace Danby like to collect?
questions.explanation: His love for books motivates him to steal once every year.

questions.question: Why does Horace steal every year?
questions.correct_answer: To get money to buy rare books
questions.explanation: He steals only enough to last for a year and satisfy his passion for books.

questions.question: Where does the robbery take place?
questions.explanation: Horace plans to rob the safe located in the house at Shotover Grange.

questions.question: Who is the young lady Horace meets in the house?
questions.correct_answer: A thief pretending to be the owner’s wife
questions.explanation: She deceives Horace by confidently acting as the lady of the house.

questions.question: How does the young lady deceive Horace?
questions.correct_answer: By pretending she forgot the safe’s combination
questions.explanation: She tricks Horace into opening the safe and handing over the jewels.

questions.question: Why does Horace remove his gloves?
questions.correct_answer: To light the young lady’s cigarette
questions.explanation: This emotional mistake leads to fingerprints being left behind.

questions.question: Why is Horace finally arrested?
questions.correct_answer: His fingerprints are found on the safe
questions.explanation: Without gloves, Horace leaves evidence that proves his crime.

questions.question: Why is the title 'A Question of Trust' appropriate?
questions.correct_answer: Because misplaced trust leads to Horace’s downfall
questions.explanation: Horace blindly trusts the young lady and is betrayed.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.explanation: The story shows that dishonesty destroys trust and leads to punishment.

notes.title: Misplaced Trust
notes.content: Horace’s blind trust in the young lady becomes the main cause of his failure.

notes.title: Irony in the Story
notes.content: A professional thief is deceived and caught due to a small emotional lapse.

facts.fact: Horace Danby is arrested because of his own carelessness.
The story questions the idea of honour among thieves.
